section.name: &sect-num;. BreakTest Features
section.p: BreakTest keeps the JMX test-plan model and many JMeter-compatible names for compatibility, but it is not a frozen copy of Apache JMeter. This page summarizes the main BreakTest-specific capabilities and where they affect test design.

subsection.name: &sect-num;.1 Java 21+ runtime
subsection.p: BreakTest requires Java 21 or later. The newer baseline allows the runtime to use modern JVM behavior, including virtual-thread support where it helps concurrency and resource usage.

subsection.name: &sect-num;.2 Modern HTTP and HTTP/2
subsection.p: The built-in HTTP sampler path uses Apache HttpClient 5 and includes HTTP/2 support. Legacy HttpClient 4 jars remain bundled for plugin compatibility, but new built-in HTTP behavior should be tested against the HttpClient 5 path.
BreakTest also improves response handling with Brotli and Zstandard decoding, lazy decompression, and response retention modes that can reduce memory pressure during larger runs.

subsection.name: &sect-num;.3 Scheduling, pause/resume, and load models
subsection.p: The unified Thread Group supports closed and open workload models. Closed-model phases can combine different ramp-up speeds, hold periods, and target thread counts. Open-model helpers can model arrivals and cap active threads.

Pause and Resume are available from the GUI and command wrappers. In a closed model, pausing holds active threads instead of continuing the ramp. In an open model, new arrivals are paused and active threads finish naturally, so load drops while paused.
Shutdown is quicker than historical JMeter behavior: shutdown no longer waits for timers to complete and only waits for in-flight server responses to finish cleanly.

subsection.name: &sect-num;.4 Parallel and forked work
subsection.p: The Parallel Controller can run child samplers concurrently with bounded parallelism. The Fork Controller can start asynchronous child branches that share virtual-user context and complete before thread teardown.

subsection.name: &sect-num;.5 AI-assisted scripting
subsection.p: BreakTest includes local-agent support for AI-assisted scripting. Tools such as Codex, Claude Code, and opencode can use the BreakTest MCP server and local agent tooling to inspect a JMX file, validate it, repair selectors and correlation, parameterize dynamic values, add assertions, and rerun checks.
When used from the GUI, BreakTest can highlight changes made by the repair workflow so you can review the generated edits before continuing.

subsection.name: &sect-num;.6 GUI editing and large JMX files
subsection.p: BreakTest adds undo/redo for common semantic tree edits, background JMX loading, safe missing-plugin placeholders, and lighter loading paths for large plans. Search Tree can flag elements with pre-processors, post-processors, assertions, timers, and config elements, and can remove matching elements with confirmation.

subsection.name: &sect-num;.7 Visual debugging and HAR migration
subsection.p: View Results Tree includes richer HTTP/TLS/cookie/request/response diagnostics and source navigation. When BreakTest HAR metadata is present, recorded request/response tabs and recorded-vs-replayed diffs help debug migration and HTTP/2 replay behavior.

subsection.name: &sect-num;.8 Scripting, data, and assertions
subsection.p: BreakTest improves If and While Controller conditions with structured rows, adds transaction delay and pacing controls, supports random CSV ordering and CSV preview, and adds fail-on-no-match options across common extractors.

subsection.name: &sect-num;.9 Compatibility notes
subsection.p: Existing JMeter JMX files remain supported where practical. Many package names, properties, log file names, and internal identifiers intentionally still contain jmeter or org.apache.jmeter because changing them would break existing test plans and plugins.

BreakTest-specific JMX metadata and features may not round-trip into older Apache JMeter versions. RMI Remote Server style distributed execution has been removed; use BreakTest Enterprise or another controlled orchestration layer for distributed runs.
